_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Assess access controls based on principles of least privilege and need-to-know.
reference_document_element_description: Manage data platform access controls, including role definitions, permission grants, access reviews, and privilege escalation procedures.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-005 (SP, cosine: 0.563), DA-K-016 (DA, cosine: 0.544).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.503). Shared vocabulary: [access, controls, privilege]. DCWF-only terms [assess, based, know, least, need, principles] vs WIDAI-only terms [data, definitions, escalation, grants, manage, permission] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.885, R=0.891). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Assess access controls based on principles of least privilege and need-to-know." and "Manage data platform access controls, including role definitions, permission gra" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.680 > STS 0.503 (gap +0.177). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [access, controls, privilege] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [access, controls, privilege]. DCWF-only: [assess, based, know, least, need, principles]. WIDAI-only: [data, definitions, escalation, grants, manage, permission].

signals.detail: STS 0.503 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.177 with low STS 0.503. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
